cards.name: Professor Onyx
cards.reason: Drain-and-gain Magecraft trigger that wins the game on its own; incredible card advantage.

cards.name: Galazeth Prismari
cards.reason: Turns all your artifacts into mana for big spells; elite body and ramp.

cards.name: Beledros Witherbloom
cards.reason: Generates tokens every turn and can untap all your lands for explosive Opus plays.

cards.name: Mizzix's Mastery
cards.reason: Overload for a massive turn of casting every spell in your graveyard; pure game-ender.

cards.name: Tanazir Quandrix
cards.reason: Doubles counters on ETB and makes your team's base stats massive when attacking.

cards.name: Igneous Inspiration
cards.reason: Efficient removal that Learns; essential for access to the sideboard Lessons.

cards.name: Professor of Symbology
cards.reason: Top-tier 2-drop that ensures you never miss a land drop or utility Lesson.

cards.name: Bury in Books
cards.reason: Excellent tempo removal that puts a creature deep into the library.

cards.name: Frost Blossom
cards.reason: Provides mana acceleration and fixes for the 5-mana Opus spells.

cards.name: Closing Statement
cards.reason: Destroys any creature and puts a counter on your own for a low cost if cast in main phase.

tiers.label: Good Playables
tiers.description: Solid cards that make every deck better

cards.name: Pest Summoning
cards.reason: Essential Lesson for Witherbloom; provides bodies and lifegain fuel.

cards.name: Prismari Pledgemage
cards.reason: Solid blocker that can attack as a 3/3 frequently.

cards.name: Guiding Voice
cards.reason: Cheap targeted spell for Silverquill that triggers Magecraft and Learns.

cards.name: Field Trip
cards.reason: Ramp that Learns; keeps the cards flowing while accelerating mana.

cards.name: Rise of Extus
cards.reason: Exile removal that also brings back a creature from your graveyard.

cards.name: Environmental Sciences
cards.reason: The most important Lesson for fixing mana, though not a main-deck card.

cards.name: Biblioplex Assistant
cards.reason: Recovers a key instant or sorcery to the top of the library.

cards.name: Reject
cards.reason: Standard counterspell; fine but often the first card to be cut.

tiers.label: Sideboard / Avoid
tiers.description: Only play if desperate or as sideboard cards

cards.name: Introduction to Prophecy
cards.reason: Too expensive for a main-deck card; only take as a mid-pick Lesson.

cards.name: Fracture
cards.reason: Great in Constructed, but often misses in Limited where creatures dominate.

tiers.label: Unplayable
tiers.description: Never put these in your deck

cards.name: Secret Rendezvous
cards.reason: Giving your opponent 3 cards is a recipe for losing, regardless of your gain.
